preFix.title: Soft nougat
preFix.subtitle: Italian-style white nougat with pistachios and cranberries
preFix.excerpt: Soft nougat is made by streaming hot honey and sugar syrup into whisked egg whites, producing a dense, chewy, white sweet that sets at room temperature. Pistachios and dried cranberries are folded in for colour and texture. It is cut into bars when fully set. The method requires a sugar thermometer and a stand mixer.
preFix.sourceNotes: Nougat has a long Mediterranean confectionery history, with French nougat de Montélimar and Italian torrone being the best-known regional varieties. Torrone is documented in Italian confectionery records from at least the fifteenth century and is made from honey, egg whites, and nuts. The method of streaming hot sugar syrup into egg whites is an Italian meringue technique applied to confectionery. This recipe follows the Italian white nougat formula with a honey-dominated flavour and dried fruit and nut additions.

content.text: Nougat requires two syrups cooked simultaneously: one of honey, one of sugar and water. The honey is brought to 121 degrees C and poured first into egg whites that are already at soft peaks in the stand mixer. The sugar syrup then follows at 145 degrees C, a higher temperature that gives the nougat its body. Working quickly is essential at both stages: the nougat sets fast. Line the tin and have the nuts and fruit ready to go in before you start cooking.
Cook the syrups and whisk the whites

Line a 20 x 30 cm tin with baking paper, leaving an overhang. In a small saucepan, heat 150 g honey to 121 degrees C. Simultaneously, in a medium saucepan, cook 250 g caster sugar and 75 ml water to 145 degrees C. While both syrups cook, whisk 2 egg whites in the stand mixer to soft peaks. When the honey reaches 121 degrees C, stream it slowly into the egg whites with the mixer running on high speed, pouring down the side of the bowl. Continue whisking. When the sugar syrup reaches 145 degrees C, stream it in the same way. The mixture will thicken and go from glossy to opaque as it cools.

Continue whisking for 5 to 7 minutes until the nougat is thick, very white, and holds its shape when you lift the whisk. Add 1 tsp vanilla extract. Working quickly, fold in the pistachios and dried cranberries with a silicone spatula. Scrape into the prepared tin and smooth the top, pressing down firmly. Leave to set at room temperature for 4 hours. Lift out and cut into bars with an oiled knife. Wrap each bar in baking paper.

items.problem: The nougat is very sticky and will not set firm
items.solution: One or both syrups did not reach the correct temperature. The sugar syrup must reach 145 degrees C: below this point the nougat will not set. Use two separate thermometers or cook them consecutively rather than together if managing both at once is difficult.

The knife was cold, or the nougat was too cold and firm. Use an oiled knife and cut with a single downward press rather than sawing. Allowing the nougat to come to room temperature for 15 minutes before cutting also helps.
